content:
Today's best email:

This consolidated one-page version

https://vixra.org/abs/2004.0683
or
https://www.academia.edu/42900763/pk_mk_qk ,

which doesn't lack any necessary details, definitely demonstrates that the strong Goldbach conjecture has an (almost) trivial proof. Of course, the main problem isn't the simplicity of the solution but that it immediately leads to a proof of the inconsistency of arithmetic. So, will the Goldbach conjecture be declared a taboo subject in mathematics since dealing with it leads to a meltdown?

Note to self: if you discover an inconsistency in mathematics, quickly use it to settle a bunch of Millennium Prize problems and collect the cash before pointing out that math is broken.
Then you can cash in on all the cryptocurrencies. :grinning_face_with_smiling_eyes:
